user_outcome: A SwornAgent user can log in, inspect authoritative identity and session status, and log out without fabricated account data, provider-key changes, model claims, or notification side effects.
rationale: The current device flow parses server tier and expiry but returns only token and email, after which the CLI invents a free tier and 24-hour expiry. This slice consumes C-02, owns C-03, narrows the account command to identity/session status, and binds a newly issued token to the issuer that created it.
in_scope: Return and persist server-authored token, email, optional tier, expiry, and canonical issuer from the device flow instead of manufacturing tier or lifetime locally.
Validate C-03 before the first request, announce a non-default issuer, reject unsafe redirects, and ensure no existing bearer credential is attached to device-code or polling requests.
Make account-session-v1 the sole login-status authority and trigger S01's shared migration routine before any account-aware read or write.
Render sworn account as identity/session status only: login state and email, with tier and expiry shown only when supplied by the issuer.

Treat missing and expired sessions as successful status queries with explicit login guidance; treat corrupt, conflicting, or unsupported records as non-zero recovery cases without printing record contents.
Make logout idempotently remove only account.json and any account-only transient state while preserving credentials.json and legacy notification fields byte-for-byte.
Remove credit commerce and notification configuration from the account command surface; deeper credit and notification implementation removal remains in their owning slices.
out_of_scope: Refresh tokens, browser SSO sessions, hosted tenancy, account mutation, billing, and entitlement enforcement are not implemented.
Account state does not select a model route; registry enforcement belongs to S03-direct-routing-only.
Credit cache, TUI, MCP, provider-quota messages, and API-document removal belong to S04-remove-managed-inference-credits.
The replacement webhook command and notifications.json belong to S05-independent-webhook-consent.

effort_complexity.rationale: The desired issuer and status semantics are clear, but login, persistence, output states, redirect policy, migration, and command tests span several existing surfaces.

risks.description: An issuer override becomes a way to forward an existing bearer credential.
risks.mitigation: Construct device-flow requests from an empty auth context, ban general account API base overrides, bind the returned token to the canonical issuer, and inject a canary old token in the real HTTP test.

risks.description: Account status leaks tokens or presents local guesses as server facts.
risks.mitigation: Use explicit output fields from the safe account projection only; canary-scan stdout/stderr and omit absent tier/expiry rather than defaulting them.

risks.description: Logout still removes another domain through a legacy shared-file path.
risks.mitigation: Drive built login/account/logout against hashed provider and notification fixtures and assert only account-owned files change.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the built binary completes sworn login against a loopback device issuer THE SYSTEM SHALL persist the issuer-returned email, optional tier, expiry, token, and canonical issuer in C-02 without storing a manufactured tier or expiry (C-02, C-03, N-08).

IF SWORN_AUTH_URL contains non-loopback HTTP, userinfo, a query, a fragment, a malformed host, or a redirect outside the canonical issuer THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL reject login before persisting an account record or sending an existing bearer token (C-03, N-07, N-08).

WHEN sworn account reads a valid C-02 record THE SYSTEM SHALL print logged-in state and email plus only issuer-supplied tier and expiry, and SHALL print no credit, model, provider, webhook, notification, or token field (C-02, N-02, N-03, N-08).
WHEN C-02 is absent or expired THE SYSTEM SHALL make sworn account print the corresponding not-logged-in or expired status with login guidance and exit zero (C-02, N-08).

IF C-02 is malformed, conflicting, or unsupported THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL make sworn account and account-aware login/logout fail non-zero with secret-safe doctor guidance while provider-only commands remain unaffected (C-01, C-02, N-01, N-08, N-09).

WHEN the built binary runs sworn logout after login THE SYSTEM SHALL remove only C-02, preserve credentials.json and unmigrated notification fields byte-for-byte, print Logged out, and remain idempotent on a second invocation (C-01, C-02, N-01).
